summary:
Maternal mortality is highest in Eisenmenger’s complex due to severe pulmonary hypertension and right-to-left shunting causing catastrophic hemodynamic deterioration during pregnancy. This condition leads to uncontrolled hypoxemia and rapid decompensation under increased physiological demands of gestation.  

Note: Do not include any additional information beyond what was provided in Agent2's response.

Okay, I need to summarize only the key clinical points from Agent2's reasoning about why maternal mortality is highest in certain heart diseases.

The core point revolves around Eisenmenger’s complex being associated with the worst outcomes — specifically its cause of irreversible pulmonary hypertension and right-to-left shunting. These lead to dangerous circulation problems where oxygen levels drop severely (uncontrolled hypoxemia) and patients can suddenly deteriorate when their body needs more blood flow during pregnancy.

I'll keep it strictly within two sentences as required:

Eisenmenger’s complex has the highest maternal mortality rate among heart diseases due to irreversible pulmonary hypertension and right-to-left shunting. It causes fatal complications such as uncontrolled hypoxemia and acute decompensation during pregnancy-induced stress.
